label: About
title: About Cutaway City of Warm Windows
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 49 recent saved-signal files and 75 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows around @simongerman600: This is wonderful. This page turns New York into a massive, zoomable,... and Dave Krugman’s ‘WINDOWS’ Captures a Multitudinous Portrait of New York City, plus 7 more. A source image from thisiscolossal.com helped set the palette, contrast, edge behavior, and gesture. The scene became Cutaway City of Warm Windows: Architectural section as visible plate grammar: a single cutaway isometric city slab at night, seen from a high oblique camera, with block-by-block terraces, roof planes, street canyons, river-black bands, and dense warm window apertures set into a deep charcoal field. After the image was made, a plate-reading pass mapped 9 visible regions and the mask audit tightened them against jagged glowing roof aperture, large lit glass bay, and circular roof groove.

typography.body_family: 'DFE Newsreader', Iowan Old Style, Georgia, serif
typography.rationale: Fraunces gives the title an organic, seed-label quality; Newsreader keeps the explanation calm and readable.
